political.s: <cite index="1-1">PayPal spent over $800,000 on lobbying in the first half of 2024</cite>. The company engages in fintech-sector lobbying but has not reported outside spending in the 2024 election cycle.

charity.s: <cite index="11-1">PayPal facilitates charitable giving through its PayPal Giving Fund, which allows nonprofits to process donations without fees or deductions</cite>. <cite index="16-12">Since 2018, PayPal Giving Fund has raised $4B USD to benefit charities globally</cite>. The company partners with humanitarian organizations and supports disaster relief.
charity.focus: Charitable giving infrastructure, humanitarian organizations, disaster relief

environment.s: <cite index="21-6">Since 2019, PayPal has reduced its carbon by 79%</cite>. <cite index="21-25">Since 2021, PayPal has sourced 100% of its global data center energy from renewable sources</cite>. <cite index="21-3">PayPal tracks progress toward its goal of achieving net-zero GHG emissions by 2040</cite>.

labor.s: <cite index="35-5,35-6">PayPal has an employee rating of 3.4 out of 5 stars, rated highest on Work Life Balance (3.8/5) and lowest on Management (2.8/5)</cite>. Employee reviews cite work-life balance benefits but concerns about layoffs, management quality, and advancement opportunities.

dei.s: <cite index="45-1">PayPal agreed to a $30 million settlement with the U.S. Department of Justice over a 2020 investment initiative designed to support Black and minority-owned businesses</cite>. <cite index="49-1,49-3,49-4">PayPal removed almost all references to diversity, equity, and inclusion in its annual report to shareholders, cutting most references to diversity from its human capital section</cite>.

animals.s: Not applicable - PayPal is a financial services/digital payments company with no animal testing practices.

guns.s: Not applicable - PayPal is a digital payments platform that processes transactions but does not manufacture or directly sell firearms.

privacy.s: <cite index="54-1">PayPal maintains technical, physical, and administrative security measures designed to provide protection for personal information against loss, misuse, unauthorized access, disclosure, and alteration</cite>. However, <cite index="58-26">PayPal's privacy policy expressly states it may share users' personal information with third parties</cite>, and the company collects extensive personal and financial data.

execPay.s: <cite index="68-1">PayPal Holdings Inc disclosed its CEO pay was 69 times its median employee's pay for the fiscal year ending in 2024</cite>. <cite index="62-1">CEO Alex Chriss received $1.33M in base salary, $23.37M in stock award, and $552.85K in other compensation, making $25.24M in total compensation in 2025</cite>.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
